    Tata Punch

    4.7
    User Rating (274)
    Rate & Win

    The Tata Punch gets a new 1.2 iTurbo engine (another addition to Tata's growing set of newly developed engines) and a CNG automatic with paddle shifters. It scored five stars in BNCAP crash tests. With a keen focus on first car buyers, Tata has introduced a 360-degree camera, a 10.25-inch infotainment screen, wireless Android Auto and Apple CarPlay, extended thigh support, a height-adjustable driver seat, and 366 litres of boot space.

    Top Things to Know About Tata Punch

    • Highlights
    • Walkthrough
    • Highlights
    • Walkthrough

    At the rear, the Punch gets a connected tail light, rear wing and a rear-wiper.

    The Punch Facelift gets cleaner dual-tone dashboard with a 10.25-inch infotainment screen.

    It gets paddle shifters in select AMT variants.

    The new Tata Punch 2026 gets a dual-tone blue and grey interior trim colour.

    The sunroof can be opened by using voice commands.

    The new Punch gets a 360-degree camera system.

    The new 1.2-litre iTurbo petrol engine makes 118bhp and 170Nm.

    Tata Punch facelift scored five-star BNCAP rating. Tata also simulated a head-on scenario to showcase real-world situation.

    The CNG Punch facelift gets a new five-speed AMT gearbox along with the manual transmission option.

    Includes new connected LED tail lamps, rear spoiler, shark fin antenna and a redesigned skid plate section.

    It gets the new TrailCrest R16 diamond cut alloy wheels and a ground clearance of 193mm. Doors offer a 90-degree opening.

    It has the new two-spoke illuminated steering wheel with mounted controls for audio, phone, and cruise control.

    Equipped with a 10.25-inch digital instrument cluster and tyre pressure monitoring as standard.

    There's a 1.2-litre petrol with a CNG option, both with manual and AMT gearboxes, and a 1.2-litre turbo-petrol with a six-speed gearbox.

    The standard Punch's boot space is 366-litres, but the CNG variant provides a reasonable 210-litres due to the under-boot cylinder placement.

    The Punch has auto climate control via the new AC control panel and rear AC vents provide cooling for all occupants.

    Equipped with 65W Type-C front and 15W rear charging ports, along with a wireless smartphone charger.

    The comfortable seats get new upholstery, extended thigh supports and a sliding centre console armrest with storage.

    The height-adjustable driver seat allows for a customised driving position, aiding visibility of the surroundings.

    Includes a 185/70 R15 steel spare wheel, ensuring readiness for emergencies across all variants.

    Tata Punch Latest Updates

    • Tata Punch has entered the seven lakh sales club with four years and three months of its launch.

      1 Feb 2026

    • The new Tata Punch has achieved a five-star BNCAP certification. It scored 30.58 out of 32 points for adult occupant protection and 45 out of 49 in child occupant protection.

      21 Jan 2026

    • The Tata Punch facelift has been launched at Rs. 5.59 lakh, going all the way up to Rs. 9.29 lakh (ex-showroom prices). Bookings open today.

      13 Jan 2026

    How is the Tata Punch car?

    • Pros

      • Refreshed exterior design retains the SUV character
      • It achieved a five-star BNCAP rating, and gets six airbags as standard
      • Enhanced experience with refreshed screens, AC controls, Type-C ports and more
      • New 1.2 turbo petrol is more exciting and 'Punchy', CNG AMT convenience
      • Adequately spacious and airy cabin, absorbent ride quality

    • Cons

      • Exterior noises are clearly audible inside the cabin
      • Handling is not tuned for spirited high speed cornering
      • Rear seat does not get 60:40 split folding
      • Tall people can have restrictive headroom
      • Three large adults will be a tight fit at the rear

    Tata Punch Facelift Convenience and Technology

    4.8
    User Rating (274)
    • The new Tata Punch facelift dashboard features a 10.25-inch infotainment touchscreen and a seven-inch instrument cluster, while the lower variants will feature an eight-inch infotainment display.
    • It gets a two-spoke steering wheel with the Tata logo, steering-mounted controls, a height-adjustable driver seat, 45+ iRA connected car tech, and a push start button.
    • Tata has also added a 360-degree camera system, wireless charger, Type-C fast charger, wireless Android Auto and Apple CarPlay, voice-operated sunroof, automatic climate control, and rear armrests and AC vents.
    • The Punch facelift has a boot space of 366 litres, while the CNG versions have a boot capacity of 210 litres.
    Read More
    +91
    Please enter the correct contact number.

    Tata Punch facelift Safety

    • The Punch facelift gets six airbags, ESP, iTPMS, cruise control, and all-wheel discs, ISOFIX child seat mounts.
    • It also features a 360-degree Surround View camera, an HD reverse camera, cruise control, an air purifier, and a blind-spot monitor.
    • The Tata Punch facelift scored a five-star rating in BNCAP crash safety tests. Tata also showcased its safety via a simulated rear-life vehicle-to-vehicle head-on collision with a Tata truck.
    Read More

    Tata Punch facelift Interior and Exterior Styling

    4.8
    User Rating (274)
    • The updated Punch bears a sleeker look than the outgoing model, now with 193mm ground clearance.
    • It features a revised grille, split headlights, an updated bumper, and DRLs. It will get a new rear bumper, spoiler, and tweaked LED taillights.
    • On the side, it will get redesigned 15 and 16-inch alloy wheels and a generally cleaner design language.
    • At the back, it features connected tail lights, rear wiper, spoiler and a redesigned new bumper.
    • It gets four new colour options, Cyantafic, Caramel, Bengal Rouge and Coorg Clouds.
    • Internally, it features a two-spoke steering wheel with an illuminated Tata logo, digital instrument cluster, larger infotainment screen, haptic HVAC controls, auto climate control, wireless charger, and sunroof.
    • The Tata Punch facelift gets a new blue and gray interior trim colour option.
    Read More

    Tata Punch i-CNG First Look

    By Ninad Ambre25 Feb 2023

    The Tata Punch is currently one of the best-selling models from the carmaker's portfolio. We believe a CNG-powered version would make its case stronger and guess what? It is coming soon. It will share its engine option with the Altroz. Likewise, it will be made available with a similar twin-cylinder kit in the boot. Our first impressions of the premium hatchback are out here, and we shall now delve into the details of the new Punch i-CNG. Before we begin, let us bring to your notice that the one displayed at the 2023 Auto Expo looked identical to the standard Punch. The only difference between the two in terms of looks was the ‘i-CNG’ badge on the boot.

    FAQs About Tata Punch

    Price
    Q: What is the avg ex-showroom price of Tata Punch base model?
    The avg ex-showroom price of Tata Punch base model is Rs. 5.65 Lakh which includes a registration cost of Rs. 67729, insurance premium of Rs. 30541 and additional charges of Rs. 500.
    Q: What is the avg ex-showroom price of Tata Punch top model?
    The avg ex-showroom price of Tata Punch top model is Rs. 10.60 Lakh which includes a registration cost of Rs. 102369, insurance premium of Rs. 43662 and additional charges of Rs. 500.
    Performance
    Q: What is the real world mileage of Tata Punch?
    As per users, the mileage came to be 19 kmpl in the real world.
    Specifications
    Q: What is the seating capacity in Tata Punch?
    Tata Punch is a 5 seater car.
    Q: What are the dimensions of Tata Punch?
    The dimensions of Tata Punch include its length of 3876 mm, width of 1742 mm and height of 1615 mm. The wheelbase of the Tata Punch is 2445 mm.
    Features
    Q: Does Tata Punch get a sunroof?
    Yes, 10 out of 26 variants of Tata Punch have Sunroof.
    Q: Does Tata Punch have cruise control?
    Yes, 22 out of 26 variants of Tata Punch have cruise control function. With the Cruise control enabled you can take your foot off the accelerator and move at a fixed speed constantly provided the road system permits this.
    Safety
    Q: How many airbags does Tata Punch get?
    The top Model of Tata Punch has 6 airbags. The Punch has driver, front passenger, 2 curtain, driver side and front passenger side airbags.
    Q: Does Tata Punch get ABS?
    Yes, all variants of Tata Punch have ABS. ABS is a great accident prevention technology, allowing drivers to steer while braking hard.
